prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU is like the brain of your computer. It's that special little chip inside that thinks about all the things you want your computer to do, whether you're playing those colorful games or writing your school papers. When you click on something, the CPU figures out what needs to happen next, just like how I need to think about the steps when I'm baking your favorite cookies. It works very hard and can get quite warm sometimes—that's why computers have those little fans inside! I may not understand all the technical bits and bobs about computers, but I know that CPU must be mighty smart to make everything work so quickly for you.
